steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence shows the tension Dillard observes (delicate fins vs. unsettling reproductive habits) and her struggle to reconcile these juxtapositions. The second sentence states her final synthesis: intricacy and harshness are inextricably linked. The blank must signal a final conclusion or resolution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Ultimately,’: signals a final conclusion or end-state — exactly fits Dillard's final reconciliation of the tension. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘To that end,’: implies a purposeful means toward a goal, not a wrap-up of competing ideas. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Hence,’: implies a tight logical consequence from immediate premises, too mechanistic for a philosophical resolution. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Moreover,’: simply adds another point — does not signal resolution. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
